rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Ni-catalyzed allylation/alkenylation of β-ketoesters with 1,1-diphenylethylene (and allyl-TMS) via anodic oxidation generating carbon radical intermediates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Ni(II)-enolate complex generates an α-carbonyl radical (radical cation intermediate); cathode reduces protons to H2 and assists desilylation/turnover

key_intermediates.name: Anodically oxidized Ni-bound α-carbonyl radical cation (B')
Ni(II)-bound C-C coupled alkyl intermediate after addition to allyl-TMS (C')
β-silyl carbocation (D') after second anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Ni(II) binds the β-ketoester enolate and is oxidized at the anode to a Ni-stabilized α-carbonyl radical/radical cation; this carbon radical adds to the alkene (1,1-diphenylethylene or allyl-TMS), and a second anodic oxidation yields a β-silyl carbocation that loses TMS+ (as TMSOH/TMS2O) to give the alkenylated product, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 not formed; TEMPO-adduct 50 isolated in 82% yield (HRMS [M+Na]+ 368.1831), supporting a carbon radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 not formed; BHT-adduct 51 isolated in 75% yield (HRMS [M+Na]+ 431.2183), consistent with radical trapping

probe_reagents.name: No current control
probe_reagents.observation: Without electrolysis under otherwise standard conditions, product 3 was not detected (0% yield), confirming requirement for anodic oxidation

notes: Mechanism (Scheme S3) is specifically proposed for the allyl-TMS variant forming product 31, invoking a radical-polar crossover where a β-silyl cation D' is intercepted by desilylation. For the 1,1-diphenylethylene case, only radical trapping evidence is shown; no DFT, CV, or KIE data provided. Cathodic chemistry includes H2 evolution and assistance in TMS removal via TMSOH/TMS2O.
